text.title: Agentic AI Product Ops — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for OPERATING autonomous agentic AI systems in production: machine-verifiable goal states, escalation-first runbooks, enumerated autonomous actions, behavioural regression on model bumps, agentic unit metrics on the ops dashboard.

rule.statement: Every running agent MUST report against a machine-verifiable goal state (concrete predicate). Free-form "the agent handled it" is rejected by the ops dashboard.

rationale.source: Anthropic Agent design patterns; ops post-mortems on autonomous systems

rule.rationale: Without a verifiable goal predicate, ops cannot tell whether the agent succeeded or merely returned a confident-sounding response.

rule.statement: An escalation runbook (named human, named channel, named SLAs, named triggers) MUST exist BEFORE traffic is routed to the agent. Runbooks written after first incident are rejected.

rationale.source: 40% of agentic pilots fail to productionize due to missing escalation; production incident post-mortems

rule.rationale: Escalation cannot be improvised mid-incident. The runbook is the single artefact that lets ops trust the agent in production.

rule.statement: Every autonomous action the agent may take is enumerated with a daily / weekly budget (max refunds, max emails sent, max records modified). Unbudgeted actions are rejected.

rationale.source: Blast-radius limitation patterns; rate-limit-as-safety operations literature

rule.rationale: An autonomous action without a budget is a tail-risk waiting to happen. Budgets are the operating-time equivalent of escalation in design-time.

rule.statement: Ops MUST run behavioural-regression tests when the underlying model version changes (silent provider update or explicit bump) BEFORE traffic shifts. Auto-roll-forward without regression is rejected.

rationale.source: Silent-update incident reports; behavioural drift literature

rule.rationale: Model providers update without code releases. Without regression tests, users detect incidents before operators do.

rule.statement: The ops dashboard MUST surface goal-achievement-rate, autonomy-ratio, cost-per-task, escalation-rate, and behavioural-regression status. CTR / session-length / cost-per-user are not accepted as primary agent ops metrics.

rationale.source: Agent unit-economics analyses; ops dashboards for autonomous systems

rule.rationale: Agentic systems scale with task volume, not seat count. Ops dashboards built for SaaS measure the wrong axis.

example.p: Production support agent dashboard: goal-achievement-rate 78%, autonomy-ratio 62%, cost-per-task $0.39, escalation-rate 12% (within SLA), behavioural regression: green (claude-opus-4.6@2026-04-01 vs @2026-03-01).
"The agent CTR is 31% and we get 4 escalations a week." — wrong metric axis; no goal predicate; no regression status; ops cannot tell if the agent is healthy.
